evals.prompt: I want to add one-time payments to my app so users can buy a lifetime access plan for $99. Set up Stripe Checkout.
evals.expected_output: Agent creates checkout session with one-time payment mode, correct metadata with order_id, idempotency_key, success_url and cancel_url. Webhook handler for checkout.session.completed with signature verification. DB schema additions (webhook_events table).

evals.prompt: Add monthly and yearly subscription plans to my SaaS. Monthly is $29, yearly is $199. Users should be able to see their current subscription status.
evals.expected_output: Agent creates Stripe Customer on first checkout, subscription Checkout Session, handles customer.subscription.created/updated/deleted webhook events, syncs subscription status to DB. Frontend shows current plan pulled from DB (not from Stripe API directly).

evals.prompt: How do I make sure I don't process the same Stripe webhook twice? Our server sometimes gets the same event multiple times.
evals.expected_output: Agent explains Stripe's at-least-once delivery guarantee. Implements WebhookEvent table with Stripe event ID as primary key. Shows the idempotency check at the top of the webhook handler: if event already exists in DB, return 200 immediately without reprocessing.
